content.full_text:
July 21, 1977

Frederick J. Whisman
Executive Officer
City Hall 480
San Francisco, CA

Dear Mr. Whisman:

     Thank you for your letter of July 5, 1977, regarding
the requirements of the Political Reform Act.

     The Political Reform Act requires that judges of
courts of record file financial disclosure statements
pursuant to Government Code Section 87200.  Court commissioners and other court personnel, of any court, are not
required to file Statements of Economic Interest pursuant
to that Section.  Furthermore, Government Code Section 82041
specifically exempts courts or any agency in the judicial
branch of government from the requirements of adopting Conflict of Interests Codes, which require officials of the
agency to file financial disclosure statements.

     I hope this has answered your question.  If you need
further assistance, please contact this office.

                                        Sincerely,



                                        Dawn Wiser
                                        Technical Assistance and
                                            Analysis Division

DW/gt


bcc:  Jeanne P.

FREDERICK J. WHISMAN
EXECUTIVE OFFICER
CITY HALL 480
(415) 558-3169

Superior Court of California
San Francisco

July 5, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Attention: Dawn Wiser, Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

Gentlemen:

The copy of your letter of 12/14/76 to Commissioner
Dana of Los Angeles was forwarded to this office. Will you
please advise if the same rule of that letter opinion applies
to commissioners and other court personnel, exclusive of
judges of course, of the San Francisco Superior Court.

Thank you.

Yours truly,

FREDERICK J. WHISMAN
Executive Officer

FJW:te
Enclosure

cc: Honorable Lawrence S. Mana

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET-BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                                322-5501                              322-6441

December 14, 1976

Franklin E. Dana
Commissioner, Dept. 1
111 North Hill
Los Angeles, CA    90012

Dear Mr. Dana:

     As I indicated in our telephone conversation on December 10, 1976, this letter is to confirm the fact that Commissioners of the court are not required to file Statements of Economic Interests pursuant to the Political Reform Act.

     If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Dawn Wiser
                                        Technical Assistance and
                                        Analysis Division

sections.question: Will you please advise if the same rule of that letter opinion applies to commissioners and other court personnel, exclusive of judges of course, of the San Francisco Superior Court.
sections.conclusion: The Political Reform Act requires that judges of courts of record file financial disclosure statements pursuant to Government Code Section 87200. Court commissioners and other court personnel, of any court, are not required to file Statements of Economic Interest pursuant to that Section. Furthermore, Government Code Section 82041 specifically exempts courts or any agency in the judicial branch of government from the requirements of adopting Conflict of Interests Codes, which require officials of the agency to file financial disclosure statements.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether court commissioners and other court personnel at the San Francisco Superior Court are subject to the same financial disclosure requirements as judges under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Court commissioners and other court personnel (excluding judges) are not required to file Statements of Economic Interest. Courts are exempt from adopting Conflict of Interest Codes that would otherwise require such filings.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (July 5, 1977 letter from Whisman) and FPPC response letter (July 21, 1977 from Wiser). Also includes reference correspondence from December 14, 1976 to Commissioner Dana on same topic. Explicit question and conclusion sections extracted from the primary FPPC response letter.

QUESTION: Will you please advise if the same rule of that letter opinion applies to commissioners and other court personnel, exclusive of judges of course, of the San Francisco Superior Court.

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act requires that judges of courts of record file financial disclosure statements pursuant to Government Code Section 87200. Court commissioners and other court personnel, of any court, are not required to file Statements of Economic Interest pursuant to that Section. Furthermore, Government Code Section 82041 specifically exempts courts or any agency in the judicial branch of government from the requirements of adopting Conflict of Interests Codes, which require officials of the agency to file financial disclosure statements.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms that judges of courts of record must file financial disclosure statements, but court commissioners and other court personnel are exempt from this requirement under the Political Reform Act.
